q: What does Nietzsche mean by the will to power? Answer using only the knowledge base and quote it verbatim in the source language.

judge.note: The answer states that will to power is life’s drive to exert/manifest strength and that self-preservation is only indirect; it does not fully and substantively state the non-political scope beyond noting it is not merely political thirst, nor does it clearly include the knowledge/philosophy aspect as required.
judge.raw: {"facts_covered":[1,2],"note":"The answer states that will to power is life’s drive to exert/manifest strength and that self-preservation is only indirect; it does not fully and substantively state the non-political scope beyond noting it is not merely political thirst, nor does it clearly include the knowledge/philosophy aspect as required."}

confusion.note: No attribution/quoting errors vs the key detected; the answer correctly attributes will-to-power to Nietzsche and does not merge different thinkers’ views.

trace.final_text:
According to the knowledge base, Nietzsche’s “will to power” means that life is fundamentally not about self-preservation but about expressing and expanding force.

The clearest formulation is:

> “Прежде всего нечто живое хочет проявлять свою силу — сама жизнь есть воля к власти: самосохранение есть только одно из косвенных и мног...”  
> (“First of all, living things want to manifest their strength — life itself is will to power: self-preservation is only one of the indirect and many …”)

The knowledge base also states:

> “Жизнь есть воля к власти, а не самосохранение”  
> (“Life is will to power, not self-preservation”)

And it adds that this is “не политическая «жажда власти»” — not a political “thirst for power.”

So, based only on the retrieved material, Nietzsche’s will to power is the idea that life’s basic drive is to проявлять свою силу — to manifest its strength — rather than merely preserve itself.
